Document:
[color=#897236][mono]──── [color=#FFA500][bold]V. Chain of Command[/bold][/color] ───────────────────────────────[/mono][/color]
The following section outlines the TDF chain of command:
[bold]Chief Enforcer:[/bold] Commander of the TDF and all associated security forces. The Chief Enforcer holds overall authority over security operations, enforcement actions, and command decisions.
[bold]Warden:[/bold] Executive officer to the Chief Enforcer. The Warden is responsible for ensuring that prisoners are held, processed, and treated in accordance with standard operating procedure.
[bold]Patrol Team Leader:[/bold] Leads assigned patrol units during field operations. The Patrol Team Leader is responsible for coordinating enforcers, maintaining patrol discipline, and reporting operational developments to higher command.
[bold]Enforcer:[/bold] Standard law enforcement officers within the TDF. Enforcers carry out patrols, arrests, security duties, and lawful enforcement actions.
[bold]Junior Enforcer:[/bold] Entry-level troopers still learning TDF procedures, expectations, and field operations. Junior Enforcers operate under the supervision of higher-ranking personnel.
NEXT PAGE --> [textlink="VI. Command Authority and Privileges" link="SOPCommandAuthority"]
